newsletters.subject: Why we're at the beginning of the AI hardware boom | Caitlin Kalinowski (ex-OpenAI, Meta, Apple)
newsletters.author: Lenny Rachitsky
newsletters.wiki_relevance: AI hardware/infrastructure boom featuring Caitlin Kalinowski, a key hardware executive with experience at OpenAI (leading robotics & hardware), Meta (VR/AR hardware), and Apple (Mac Pro). Directly relevant to LLM inference/training infrastructure and the AI hardware ecosystem.
newsletters.summary: Podcast interview with Caitlin Kalinowski discussing why we're at the beginning of an AI hardware boom. Covers hardware challenges in AI training/inference, supply chain dynamics, and the hardware-software co-design imperative for next-gen AI systems.
action_items: Create entity page for Caitlin Kalinowski (ex-OpenAI/Meta/Apple hardware executive)

Enrich existing inference-hardware or training-infrastructure concept pages

newsletters.subject: Anthropic Pulls Away, OpenAI Strikes Back, and Google's Gemini Rising
newsletters.author: Alex Banks
newsletters.wiki_relevance: Direct coverage of competitive dynamics between the three major LLM providers: Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google (Gemini). Core wiki territory — LLM ecosystem, model comparison, competitive landscape. High actionability for existing entity and concept pages.
newsletters.summary: Analysis of the current state of competition between Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google in the LLM space. Covers Anthropic's recent advances pulling ahead, OpenAI's counter-moves, and Google Gemini's rising position. Likely covers model capabilities, benchmarks, pricing, and strategic positioning.

newsletters.subject: The Agentic Economy Has No Black Box
newsletters.author: Unknown (Beehiiv — need canonical URL to identify)
newsletters.canonical_url_notes: Beehiiv tracking links only (link.mail.beehiiv.com). Web search found similar articles on agentic economy/observability themes: Tim O'Reilly's 'The Missing Mechanisms of the Agentic Economy' (oreillyradar.substack.com), 'The Black Box Problem' (digitalworkforce.work), and others. Exact canonical URL requires further resolution — possible digest format linking to multiple articles.
newsletters.wiki_relevance: Directly addresses AI agent observability, the 'black box' problem in agentic AI, and the missing infrastructure for the agentic economy. Highly relevant to AI agent technologies, MCP ecosystem, agent payments/economics. Touches on key themes: agent observability, MCP extension architectures, agent payments (x402, Stripe), organic search for agents, and quality/governance of agent skills.
newsletters.summary: Explores the critical missing infrastructure for the agentic economy — primarily the observability and transparency gap ('no black box'). Likely covers agent tracing, payment/financial rails for agents (MCP payments, x402 protocol), organic search/discovery for agent services, and governance mechanisms. Appears to be a comprehensive survey of the infrastructure layer needed for autonomous AI agents to participate in the real economy.
